arguments.task: Open official Front pages needed to verify API/auth/pricing/MCP fields. Read canonical first-party pages for developer API overview/reference, authentication methods, pricing or plans, and any official MCP/server docs if present. Return a concise evidence-backed summary with the exact live canonical URLs visited and key quotes/paraphrases for: one-line description, documented auth methods, access tier/gating, API surface type and breadth, rate limits, and whether an official MCP exists. Do not use third-party sources or search snippets; browse within front.com and official docs only.

Open Front official developer documentation. Find and read the API overview/reference, authentication documentation, and rate limit documentation. Return the canonical URLs visited plus concise extracted facts on API type, breadth, auth methods documented, and rate limits.

Open Front official pricing/plans page and determine whether API access appears self-serve free, trial, paid plan required, admin approval required, or partner/contact-sales gated. Return the canonical URL visited and the exact evidence used.

input.thought: Gather official Front documentation and pricing evidence in parallel from first-party pages.

raw_result.description: Customer operations platform with a collaborative inbox and APIs for managing conversations, contacts, channels, analytics, and related workflow data.
raw_result.auth_notes: Front's authentication docs describe OAuth for public integrations and API tokens for internal integrations/testing; both use Bearer tokens in the Authorization header. MCP docs separately note OAuth 2.1 with PKCE for the MCP server flow.
raw_result.gating_reason: Front's pricing page offers self-serve 'Try for Free' access, but no permanent free tier; API access is tied to paid plans/trials shown on the pricing table.

api_surface.breadth: Official Core API documentation covers conversations, messages, contacts, users, channels/plugins, analytics, tags, webhooks, and related mailbox operations.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Front documents an official MCP server in open beta at mcp.frontapp.com/mcp, with per-user identity and OAuth 2.1 + PKCE authentication.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Rate limits are documented by plan: Starter 50 rpm, Professional 100 rpm, Enterprise 200 rpm; partner OAuth integrations 120 rpm per company; configured servers 600 rpm. Docs also mention 429 responses, retry-after, and x-ratelimit headers.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs are detailed and current-looking, with dedicated pages for core API overview, authentication, rate limiting, and an official MCP server. Pricing explicitly lists API-related limits and a paid API rate-limit increase add-on.

result.description: Customer operations platform with a collaborative inbox and APIs for managing conversations, contacts, channels, analytics, and related workflow data.
result.auth_notes: Front's authentication docs describe OAuth for public integrations and API tokens for internal integrations/testing; both use Bearer tokens in the Authorization header. MCP docs separately note OAuth 2.1 with PKCE for the MCP server flow.
result.gating_reason: Front's pricing page offers self-serve 'Try for Free' access, but no permanent free tier; API access is tied to paid plans/trials shown on the pricing table.

api_surface.breadth: Official Core API documentation covers conversations, messages, contacts, users, channels/plugins, analytics, tags, webhooks, and related mailbox operations.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Front documents an official MCP server in open beta at mcp.frontapp.com/mcp, with per-user identity and OAuth 2.1 + PKCE authentication.

result.rate_limit_notes: Rate limits are documented by plan: Starter 50 rpm, Professional 100 rpm, Enterprise 200 rpm; partner OAuth integrations 120 rpm per company; configured servers 600 rpm. Docs also mention 429 responses, retry-after, and x-ratelimit headers.
result.notable_signals: Official docs are detailed and current-looking, with dedicated pages for core API overview, authentication, rate limiting, and an official MCP server. Pricing explicitly lists API-related limits and a paid API rate-limit increase add-on.
